title: Shatrughna Temple
tagline: Shatrughna Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Shatrughna Swamy Temple is located in Payammal in Thrissur District of Kerala, India.
overview.description: Shatrughna Swamy Temple is located in Payammal in Thrissur District of Kerala, India. This is one of the few temples in India that is dedicated to Shatrughna, youngest brother of the god Rama in the Hindu epic Ramayana. The Shatrughna Temple is the fourth temple which devotees visit when they embark on the Nalambalam (Four Temples) yatra or visit. Visiting Nalambalam is considered to be a sacred event during the Malayalam month of Karkidakam.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Vadakkumkara (Mukundapuram, Thrissur)
topPlaces.description: Vadakkumkara is a village in the Mukundapuram taluka of the Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala, with an area of 605 hectares and harbouring 2,669 households with total population of 10,407 as per the 2011 Census. The population estimate for 2023 is c. 14,300.

topPlaces.name: Aripalam
topPlaces.description: Aripalam is a village situated in Thrissur district, Kerala, India. Nearest towns are Irinjalakuda and Kodungallore.

topPlaces.name: Padiyur
topPlaces.description: Padiyur is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. Irinjalakuda and Kodungallur are the nearest towns.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Church, Cheloor
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Church is a Syro-Malabar parish church in Cheloor/Edathirinji, Kerala, India (in diocesan records Mary Immaculate Church, Cheloor).

topPlaces.name: Vellangallur Grama Panchayath
topPlaces.description: Vellangallur is a Grama Panchayath situated in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Cheloor
topPlaces.description: Cheloor is a place in Irinjalakuda municipality, Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is around 1 km from the famous Koodalmanikyam Temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shatrughna Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shatrughna Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shatrughna Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shatrughna Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shatrughna Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shatrughna Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Shatrughna Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shatrughna Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vadakkumkara (Mukundapuram, Thrissur), Aripalam, Padiyur, Vadakkumkara, Chamakunnu.
